Name: Oddish
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
If exposed to moonlight, it starts to move.
It roams far and wide at night to scatter its seeds.

Name: Gloom
Flavor-Text:
Its pistils exude an incredibly foul odor.
The horrid stench can cause fainting at a distance of 1.25 miles.

Name: Bellossom
Flavor-Text:
Plentiful in the tropics.
When it dances, its petals rub together and make a pleasant ringing sound.

Name: Tangela
Flavor-Text: Hidden beneath a tangle of vines that grows nonstop even if the vines are torn off, this Pokémon’s true appearance remains a mystery.

Name: Tangrowth
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
Tangrowth has two arms that it can extend as it pleases.
Recent research has shown that these arms are, in fact, bundles of vines.

Name: Yanma
Flavor-Text:
Its eyes can see 360 degrees without moving its head.
It won’t miss prey—even those behind it.

Name: Yanmega ex
Effects: Discard a random Energy from this Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Roselia
Flavor-Text:
Its flowers give off a relaxing fragrance.
The stronger its aroma, the healthier the Roselia is.

Name: Roserade
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: After captivating opponents with its sweet scent, it lashes them with its thorny whips.

Name: Turtwig
Flavor-Text:
It uses its whole body to photosynthesize when exposed to sunlight.
Its shell is made from hardened soil.

Name: Grotle
Flavor-Text:
It lives along water in forests.
In the daytime, it leaves the forest to sunbathe its treed shell.

Name: Torterra
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Frenzy Plant.
Flavor-Text: Ancient people imagined that beneath the ground dwelt a gigantic Torterra.

Name: Kricketot
Flavor-Text:
Its legs are short.
Whenever it stumbles, its stiff antennae clack with a xylophone-like sound.

Name: Kricketune
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: By allowing its cry to resonate in the hollow of its belly, it produces a captivating sound.

Name: Burmy
Flavor-Text: To shelter itself from cold, wintry winds, it covers itself with a cloak made of twigs and leaves.

Name: Wormadam
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text:
Its appearance changes depending on where it evolved.
The materials on hand become a part of its body.

Name: Combee
Effects: Put 1 random Basic Pokémon from your deck onto your Bench.
Flavor-Text: At night, Combee sleep in a group of about a hundred, packed closely together in a lump.

Name: Vespiquen
Flavor-Text: It houses its colony in cells in its body and releases various pheromones to make those grubs do its bidding.

Name: Carnivine
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text:
It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling saliva, then chomps down.
It takes a whole day to eat prey.

Name: Leafeon
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.
Flavor-Text: When you see Leafeon asleep in a patch of sunshine, you’ll know it is using photosynthesis to produce clean air.

Name: Mow Rotom
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The lawn mower is one of the household appliances that led to the development of the Rotom Dex.

Name: Shaymin
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may heal 10 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It can dissolve toxins in the air to instantly transform ruined land into a lush field of flowers.

Name: Magmar
Effects: Take a [R] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
Magmar dispatches its prey with fire.
But it regrets this habit once it realizes that it has burned its intended prey to a charred crisp.

Name: Magmortar
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: When Magmortar inhales deeply, the fire burning in its belly intensifies, rising in temperature to over 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Slugma
Flavor-Text: A common sight in volcanic areas, it slowly slithers around in a constant search for warm places.

Name: Magcargo
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Its brittle shell occasionally spouts intense flames that circulate throughout its body.

Name: Chimchar
Flavor-Text:
Its fiery rear end is fueled by gas made in its belly.
Even rain can’t extinguish the fire.

Name: Monferno
Flavor-Text: It skillfully controls the intensity of the fire on its tail to keep its foes at an ideal distance.

Name: Infernape ex
Effects: Discard all [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Heat Rotom
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: If the convection microwave oven is not working properly, then the Rotom inhabiting it will become lethargic.

Name: Swinub
Flavor-Text:
It rubs its snout on the ground to find and dig up food.
It sometimes discovers hot springs.

Name: Piloswine
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks from [R] or [W] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
If it charges at an enemy, the hairs on its back stand up straight.
It is very sensitive to sound.

Name: Mamoswine
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes −30 damage from attacks from [R] or [W] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
This Pokémon can be spotted in wall paintings from as far back as 10,000 years ago.
For a while, it was thought to have gone extinct.

Name: Regice
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks used by your opponent's Pokémon done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: With cold air that can reach temperatures as low as −328 degrees Fahrenheit, Regice instantly freezes any creature that approaches it.

Name: Piplup
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
It doesn’t like to be taken care of.
It’s difficult to bond with since it won’t listen to its Trainer.

Name: Prinplup
Flavor-Text:
It lives alone, away from others.
Apparently, every one of them believes it is the most important.

Name: Empoleon
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
It swims as fast as a jet boat.
The edges of its wings are sharp and can slice apart drifting ice.

Name: Buizel
Flavor-Text:
It spins its two tails like a screw to propel itself through water.
The tails also slice clinging seaweed.

Name: Floatzel
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text:
With its flotation sac inflated, it can carry people on its back.
It deflates the sac before it dives.

Name: Shellos
Flavor-Text:
It used to have a shell on its back long ago.
This species is closely related to Pokémon like Shellder.

Name: Gastrodon
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They normally inhabit rocky seashores, but in times of continuous rain, they can sometimes be found in the mountains, far from the sea.

Name: Finneon
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
The line running down its side can store sunlight.
It shines vividly at night.

Name: Lumineon
Flavor-Text:
With its shining light, it lures its prey close.
However, the light also happens to attract ferocious fish Pokémon—its natural predators.

Name: Snover
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a [F] Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text:
During cold seasons, it migrates to the mountain’s lower reaches.
It returns to the snow-covered summit in the spring.

Name: Abomasnow
Flavor-Text:
It lives a quiet life on mountains that are perpetually covered in snow.
It hides itself by whipping up blizzards.

Name: Glaceon
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text:
It can control its body temperature at will.
This enables it to freeze the moisture in the atmosphere, creating flurries of diamond dust.

Name: Wash Rotom
Flavor-Text:
This Rotom has entered a washing machine.
It nods with satisfaction after it floods the surrounding area.

Name: Frost Rotom
Effects: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
This Rotom has entered a refrigerator.
It leaps around gleefully after it uses cold air to freeze the area around it.

Name: Palkia ex
Effects:
Discard 3 [W] Energy from this Pokémon.
This attack also does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Manaphy
Effects:
Choose 2 of your Benched Pokémon.
For each of those Pokémon, take a [W] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is born with a wondrous power that lets it bond with any kind of Pokémon.

Name: Magnemite
Flavor-Text: The electromagnetic waves emitted by the units at the sides of its head expel antigravity, which allows it to float.

Name: Magneton
Flavor-Text:
Three Magnemite are linked by a strong magnetic force.
Earaches will occur if you get too close.

Name: Magnezone
Effects: Discard a [L] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: As it zooms through the sky, this Pokémon seems to be receiving signals of unknown origin while transmitting signals of unknown purpose.

Name: Voltorb
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text:
It rolls to move.
If the ground is uneven, a sudden jolt from hitting a bump can cause it to explode.

Name: Electrode
Flavor-Text:
The more energy it charges up, the faster it gets.
But this also makes it more likely to explode.

Name: Electabuzz
Effects: Take a [L] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Many power plants keep Ground-type Pokémon around as a defense against Electabuzz that come seeking electricity.

Name: Electivire
Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra [L] Energy attached, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text:
The amount of electrical energy this Pokémon produces is proportional to the rate of its pulse.
The voltage jumps while Electivire is battling.

Name: Shinx
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
The extension and contraction of its muscles generates electricity.
It glows when in trouble.

Name: Luxio
Flavor-Text:
Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws.
A light scratch causes fainting in foes.

Name: Luxray
Effects:
Discard all [L] Energy from this Pokémon.
This attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It can see clearly through walls to track down its prey and seek its lost young.

Name: Pachirisu ex
Effects: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 40 more damage.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Rotom
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its electricity-like body can enter some kinds of machines and take control in order to make mischief.

Name: Togepi
Flavor-Text:
The shell seems to be filled with joy.
It is said that it will share good luck when treated kindly.

Name: Togetic
Flavor-Text: They say that it will appear before kindhearted, caring people and shower them with happiness.

Name: Togekiss
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Overdrive Smash attack does +60 damage.
Flavor-Text:
These Pokémon are never seen anywhere near conflict or turmoil.
In recent times, they’ve hardly been seen at all.

Name: Misdreavus
Flavor-Text:
This Pokémon startles people in the middle of the night.
It gathers fear as its energy.

Name: Mismagius ex
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Ralts
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The horns on its head provide a strong power that enables it to sense people’s emotions.

Name: Kirlia
Flavor-Text: It has a psychic power that enables it to distort the space around it and see into the future.

Name: Duskull
Flavor-Text: If it finds bad children who won’t listen to their parents, it will spirit them away—or so it’s said.

Name: Dusclops
Flavor-Text:
It seeks drifting will-o’-the-wisps and sucks them into its empty body.
What happens inside is a mystery.

Name: Dusknoir
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may choose 1 of your Pokémon that has damage on it, and move all of its damage to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
At the bidding of transmissions from the spirit world, it steals people and Pokémon away.
No one knows whether it has a will of its own.

Name: Drifloon
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: It is whispered that any child who mistakes Drifloon for a balloon and holds on to it could wind up missing.

Name: Drifblim
Flavor-Text: Some say this Pokémon is a collection of souls burdened with regrets, silently drifting through the dusk.

Name: Uxie
Effects: Take a [P] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to Mesprit or Azelf.
Flavor-Text: Known as “The Being of Knowledge.” It is said that it can wipe out the memory of those who see its eyes.

Name: Mesprit
Effects:
You can use this attack only if you have Uxie and Azelf on your Bench.
Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Known as “The Being of Emotion.” It taught humans the nobility of sorrow, pain, and joy.

Name: Azelf
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Known as “The Being of Willpower.” It sleeps at the bottom of a lake to keep the world in balance.

Name: Giratina
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is said to live in a world on the reverse side of ours, where common knowledge is distorted and strange.

Name: Cresselia
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
Shiny particles are released from its wings like a veil.
It is said to represent the crescent moon.

Name: Rhyhorn
Flavor-Text: Strong, but not too bright, this Pokémon can shatter even a skyscraper with its charging tackles.

Name: Rhydon
Flavor-Text:
It begins walking on its hind legs after evolution.
It can punch holes through boulders with its horn.

Name: Rhyperior
Effects: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck.
Flavor-Text:
It can load up to three projectiles per arm into the holes in its hands.
What launches out of those holes could be either rocks or Roggenrola.

Name: Gligar
Flavor-Text:
It usually clings to cliffs.
When it spots its prey, it spreads its wings and glides down to attack.

Name: Gliscor
Effects:
Flip 2 coins.
This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text:
It observes prey while hanging inverted from branches.
When the chance presents itself, it swoops!

Name: Hitmontop
Flavor-Text:
It launches kicks while spinning.
If it spins at high speed, it may bore its way into the ground.

Name: Nosepass
Flavor-Text: It moves less than an inch a year, but when it’s in a jam, it will spin and drill down into the ground in a split second.

Name: Regirock
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text:
Every bit of Regirock’s body is made of stone.
As parts of its body erode, this Pokémon sticks rocks to itself to repair what’s been lost.

Name: Cranidos
Flavor-Text: A primeval Pokémon, it possesses a hard and sturdy skull, lacking any intelligence within.

Name: Rampardos
Effects: If your opponent's Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, this Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: In ancient times, people would dig up fossils of this Pokémon and use its skull, which is harder than steel, to make helmets.

Name: Wormadam
Flavor-Text:
Its appearance changes depending on where it evolved.
The materials on hand become a part of its body.

Name: Riolu
Flavor-Text:
They communicate with one another using their auras.
They are able to run all through the night.

Name: Lucario
Ability-Effect: Attacks used by your [F] Pokémon do +20 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It’s said that no foe can remain invisible to Lucario, since it can detect auras—even those of foes it could not otherwise see.

Name: Hippopotas
Flavor-Text:
It shuts its nostrils tight, then travels through sand as if walking.
They form colonies of around 10.

Name: Hippowdon
Flavor-Text:
It is surprisingly quick to anger.
It holds its mouth agape as a display of its strength.

Name: Gallade ex
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Murkrow
Flavor-Text: Feared and loathed by many, it is believed to bring misfortune to all those who see it at night.

Name: Honchkrow
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
It is merciless by nature.
It is said that it never forgives the mistakes of its Murkrow followers.

Name: Sneasel
Effects:
Flip 2 coins.
This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: This cunning Pokémon hides under the cover of darkness, waiting to attack its prey.

Name: Weavile ex
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 40 more damage.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Poochyena
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon with a persistent nature, it chases its chosen prey until the prey becomes exhausted.

Name: Mightyena
Flavor-Text:
It will always obey the commands of a skilled Trainer.
Its behavior arises from its living in packs in ancient times.

Name: Stunky
Flavor-Text:
It sprays a foul fluid from its rear.
Its stench spreads over a mile radius, driving Pokémon away.

Name: Skuntank
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text:
It attacks by spraying a horribly smelly fluid from the tip of its tail.
Attacks from above confound it.

Name: Spiritomb
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its constant mischief and misdeeds resulted in it being bound to an Odd Keystone by a mysterious spell.

Name: Skorupi
Flavor-Text:
After burrowing into the sand, it waits patiently for prey to come near.
This Pokémon and Sizzlipede share common descent.

Name: Drapion
Effects:
Flip 4 coins.
This attack does 40 damage for each heads.
If at least 2 of them are heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text:
Its poison is potent, but it rarely sees use.
This Pokémon prefers to use physical force instead, going on rampages with its car-crushing strength.

Name: Croagunk
Effects:
Flip a coin for each Pokémon you have in play.
This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Inflating its poison sacs, it fills the area with an odd sound and hits flinching opponents with a poison jab.

Name: Toxicroak
Effects:
Flip a coin for each Pokémon you have in play.
This attack does 40 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Swaying and dodging the attacks of its foes, it weaves its flexible body in close, then lunges out with its poisonous claws.

Name: Darkrai
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It chases people and Pokémon from its territory by causing them to experience deep, nightmarish slumbers.

Name: Darkrai ex
Ability-Effect: Whenever you attach a [D] Energy from your Energy Zone to this Pokémon, do 20 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Skarmory
Effects: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: People fashion swords from Skarmory’s shed feathers, so this Pokémon is a popular element in heraldic designs.

Name: Registeel
Flavor-Text: Registeel’s body is made of a strange material that is flexible enough to stretch and shrink but also more durable than any metal.

Name: Shieldon
Flavor-Text:
A mild-mannered, herbivorous Pokémon, it used its face to dig up tree roots to eat.
The skin on its face was plenty tough.

Name: Bastiodon
Ability-Effect:
If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin.
If heads, this Pokémon takes −100 damage from that attack.
Flavor-Text: The bones of its face are huge and hard, so they were mistaken for its spine until after this Pokémon was successfully restored.

Name: Wormadam
Effects:
Flip a coin until you get tails.
This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text:
Its appearance changes depending on where it evolved.
The materials on hand become a part of its body.

Name: Bronzor
Flavor-Text: Ancient people believed that the pattern on Bronzor’s back contained a mysterious power.

Name: Bronzong
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text:
In ages past, this Pokémon was revered as a bringer of rain.
It was found buried in the ground.

Name: Probopass
Effects:
Flip 3 coins.
This attack does 50 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Although it can control its units known as Mini-Noses, they sometimes get lost and don’t come back.

Name: Dialga ex
Effects: Take 2 [M] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Heatran
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text:
It dwells in volcanic caves.
It digs in with its cross-shaped feet to crawl on ceilings and walls.

Name: Gible
Flavor-Text:
It skulks in caves, and when prey or an enemy passes by, it leaps out and chomps them.
The force of its attack sometimes chips its teeth.

Name: Gabite
Flavor-Text:
In rare cases, it molts and sheds its scales.
Medicine containing its scales as an ingredient will make a weary body feel invigorated.

Name: Garchomp
Ability-Effect:
You must discard a card from your hand in order to use this Ability.
Once during your turn, you may draw a card.
Flavor-Text: It is said that when one runs at high speed, its wings create blades of wind that can fell nearby trees.

Name: Lickitung
Flavor-Text:
If this Pokémon’s sticky saliva gets on you and you don’t clean it off, an intense itch will set in.
The itch won’t go away, either.

Name: Lickilicky ex
Effects:
Flip a coin until you get tails.
This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Eevee
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment.

Name: Porygon
Flavor-Text:
State-of-the-art technology was used to create Porygon.
It was the first artificial Pokémon to be created via computer programming.

Name: Porygon2
Flavor-Text:
This is a Porygon that was updated with special data.
Porygon2 develops itself by learning about many different subjects all on its own.

Name: Porygon-Z
Effects: Change the type of the next Energy that will be generated for your opponent to 1 of the following at random: [G],[R],[W],[L],[P],[F],[D], or [M].
Flavor-Text: Porygon-Z had a program installed to allow it to move between dimensions, but the program also caused instability in Porygon-Z’s behavior.

Name: Aipom
Flavor-Text:
As it did more and more with its tail, its hands became clumsy.
It makes its nest high in the treetops.

Name: Ambipom
Effects:
Flip 2 coins.
This attack does 40 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text:
It uses its tails for everything.
If it wraps both of its tails around you and gives you a squeeze, that’s proof it really likes you.

Name: Starly
Effects: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
They flock around mountains and fields, chasing after bug Pokémon.
Their singing is noisy and annoying.

Name: Staravia
Flavor-Text:
Recognizing their own weakness, they always live in a group.
When alone, a Staravia cries noisily.

Name: Staraptor
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.
Flavor-Text:
When Staravia evolve into Staraptor, they leave the flock to live alone.
They have sturdy wings.

Name: Bidoof
Effects: Halve your opponent's Active Pokémon's remaining HP, rounded down.
Flavor-Text:
With nerves of steel, nothing can perturb it.
It is more agile and active than it appears.

Name: Bibarel
Flavor-Text: It busily makes its nest with stacks of branches and roots it has cut up with its sharp incisors.

Name: Buneary
Flavor-Text:
If both of Buneary’s ears are rolled up, something is wrong with its body or mind.
It’s a sure sign the Pokémon is in need of care.

Name: Lopunny
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
Lopunny is constantly monitoring its surroundings.
If danger approaches, this Pokémon responds with superdestructive kicks.

Name: Glameow
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text:
It claws if displeased and purrs when affectionate.
Its fickleness is very popular among some.

Name: Purugly
Effects:
Your opponent reveals their hand.
Choose a card you find there and shuffle it into your opponent's deck.
Flavor-Text: It would claim another Pokémon’s nest as its own if it finds a nest sufficiently comfortable.

Name: Chatot
Effects:
Flip 3 coins.
This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text:
It mimics the cries of other Pokémon to trick them into thinking it’s one of them.
This way they won’t attack it.

Name: Fan Rotom
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, put your opponent's Active Pokémon into their hand.
Flavor-Text:
This Rotom has entered an electric fan.
It smirks with satisfaction over a prank well pulled after it blows away everything around it.

Name: Regigigas
Effects: This attack does more damage equal to the damage this Pokémon has on it.
Flavor-Text: It is said to have made Pokémon that look like itself from a special ice mountain, rocks, and magma.

Name: Skull Fossil
Description: Play this card as if it were a 40-HP Basic [C] Pokémon.At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.This card can't retreat.

Name: Armor Fossil
Description: Play this card as if it were a 40-HP Basic [C] Pokémon.At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.This card can't retreat.

Name: Pokemon Communication
Description: Choose a Pokémon in your hand and switch it with a random Pokémon in your deck.

Name: Giant Cape
Description: The Pokémon this card is attached to gets +20 HP.

Name: Rocky Helmet
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, do 20 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.

Name: Lum Berry
Description: At the end of each turn, if the Pokémon this card is attached to is affected by any Special Conditions, it recovers from all of them, and discard this card.

Name: Cyrus
Description: Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has damage on it to the Active Spot.

Name: Team Galactic Grunt
Description: Put 1 random Glameow, Stunky, or Croagunk from your deck into your hand.

Name: Cynthia
Description: During this turn, attacks used by your Garchomp or Togekiss do +50 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

Name: Volkner
Description: Choose 1 of your Electivire or Luxray. Attach 2 [L] Energy from your discard pile to that Pokémon.

Name: Dawn
Description: Move an Energy from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.

Name: Mars
Description: Your opponent shuffles their hand into their deck and draws a card for each of their remaining points needed to win.

Name: Tangrowth
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
Tangrowth has two arms that it can extend as it pleases.
Recent research has shown that these arms are, in fact, bundles of vines.

Name: Combee
Effects: Put 1 random Basic Pokémon from your deck onto your Bench.
Flavor-Text: At night, Combee sleep in a group of about a hundred, packed closely together in a lump.

Name: Carnivine
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text:
It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling saliva, then chomps down.
It takes a whole day to eat prey.

Name: Shaymin
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may heal 10 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It can dissolve toxins in the air to instantly transform ruined land into a lush field of flowers.

Name: Mamoswine
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes −30 damage from attacks from [R] or [W] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
This Pokémon can be spotted in wall paintings from as far back as 10,000 years ago.
For a while, it was thought to have gone extinct.

Name: Gastrodon
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They normally inhabit rocky seashores, but in times of continuous rain, they can sometimes be found in the mountains, far from the sea.

Name: Manaphy
Effects:
Choose 2 of your Benched Pokémon.
For each of those Pokémon, take a [W] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is born with a wondrous power that lets it bond with any kind of Pokémon.

Name: Shinx
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
The extension and contraction of its muscles generates electricity.
It glows when in trouble.

Name: Rotom
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its electricity-like body can enter some kinds of machines and take control in order to make mischief.

Name: Drifloon
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: It is whispered that any child who mistakes Drifloon for a balloon and holds on to it could wind up missing.

Name: Mesprit
Effects:
You can use this attack only if you have Uxie and Azelf on your Bench.
Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Known as “The Being of Emotion.” It taught humans the nobility of sorrow, pain, and joy.

Name: Giratina
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is said to live in a world on the reverse side of ours, where common knowledge is distorted and strange.

Name: Cresselia
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
Shiny particles are released from its wings like a veil.
It is said to represent the crescent moon.

Name: Rhyperior
Effects: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck.
Flavor-Text:
It can load up to three projectiles per arm into the holes in its hands.
What launches out of those holes could be either rocks or Roggenrola.

Name: Lucario
Ability-Effect: Attacks used by your [F] Pokémon do +20 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It’s said that no foe can remain invisible to Lucario, since it can detect auras—even those of foes it could not otherwise see.

Name: Hippopotas
Flavor-Text:
It shuts its nostrils tight, then travels through sand as if walking.
They form colonies of around 10.

Name: Spiritomb
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its constant mischief and misdeeds resulted in it being bound to an Odd Keystone by a mysterious spell.

Name: Croagunk
Effects:
Flip a coin for each Pokémon you have in play.
This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Inflating its poison sacs, it fills the area with an odd sound and hits flinching opponents with a poison jab.

Name: Heatran
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text:
It dwells in volcanic caves.
It digs in with its cross-shaped feet to crawl on ceilings and walls.

Name: Garchomp
Ability-Effect:
You must discard a card from your hand in order to use this Ability.
Once during your turn, you may draw a card.
Flavor-Text: It is said that when one runs at high speed, its wings create blades of wind that can fell nearby trees.

Name: Staraptor
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.
Flavor-Text:
When Staravia evolve into Staraptor, they leave the flock to live alone.
They have sturdy wings.

Name: Bidoof
Effects: Halve your opponent's Active Pokémon's remaining HP, rounded down.
Flavor-Text:
With nerves of steel, nothing can perturb it.
It is more agile and active than it appears.

Name: Glameow
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text:
It claws if displeased and purrs when affectionate.
Its fickleness is very popular among some.

Name: Regigigas
Effects: This attack does more damage equal to the damage this Pokémon has on it.
Flavor-Text: It is said to have made Pokémon that look like itself from a special ice mountain, rocks, and magma.

Name: Yanmega ex
Effects: Discard a random Energy from this Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Infernape ex
Effects: Discard all [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Palkia ex
Effects:
Discard 3 [W] Energy from this Pokémon.
This attack also does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Pachirisu ex
Effects: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 40 more damage.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Mismagius ex
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Gallade ex
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Weavile ex
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 40 more damage.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Darkrai ex
Ability-Effect: Whenever you attach a [D] Energy from your Energy Zone to this Pokémon, do 20 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Dialga ex
Effects: Take 2 [M] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Lickilicky ex
Effects:
Flip a coin until you get tails.
This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Cyrus
Description: Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has damage on it to the Active Spot.

Name: Team Galactic Grunt
Description: Put 1 random Glameow, Stunky, or Croagunk from your deck into your hand.

Name: Cynthia
Description: During this turn, attacks used by your Garchomp or Togekiss do +50 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

Name: Volkner
Description: Choose 1 of your Electivire or Luxray. Attach 2 [L] Energy from your discard pile to that Pokémon.

Name: Dawn
Description: Move an Energy from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.

Name: Mars
Description: Your opponent shuffles their hand into their deck and draws a card for each of their remaining points needed to win.

Effects: Discard a random Energy from this Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.
